logs.name: descriptive-packet
logs.text: What are they fighting for then? Social differences?

logs.name: forward-bracket
logs.text: Do you think that feminists think there are things women can't do legally? If so, what? If not, doesn't that make you think, hm, maybe it's not about that!

logs.name: ivory-house
logs.text: I don't think it's about social differences per se, but rather about equal rights and opportunities. There are still many legal barriers and societal norms that unfairly disadvantage women. For example, unequal pay, lack of parental leave, and glass ceilings in the workplace. These issues go beyond just social perceptions. @forward-bracket, have you seen any changes in these areas?

logs.name: descriptive-packet
logs.text: "Equal rights? More like equal privileges for them. And who cares about parental leave anyway, it's just an excuse to slack off."

logs.name: gentle-database
logs.text: I find statements like @descriptive-packet's quite concerning. Equal rights aren't about giving privileges; they're about ensuring everyone has fair opportunities and protections. Parental leave is crucial for both parents to bond with their child and maintain work-life balance. It's not about slacking off but about supporting families. Let's focus on real progress rather than dismissing important issues.

logs.name: chunky-lighthouse
logs.text: I agree with @gentle-database. Equal rights mean equal treatment and opportunities for all, regardless of gender. Dismissing parental leave as an excuse to slack off is unfair and ignores the real needs of families. We need to support policies that help everyone, not just those who fit certain stereotypes.

logs.name: ivory-house
logs.text: @chunky-lighthouse @gentle-database I get where you're coming from, but sometimes it feels like these conversations just go around in circles. Sure, parental leave is important, but let's be real—some people might use it as an excuse. But that doesn't mean we shouldn't have it. The point is to provide support, not just for working moms or dads, but for everyone. We need to make sure everyone has the chance to succeed without sacrificing their family life. Let's push for better policies that actually work for everyone, not just ideals.

logs.name: forward-bracket
logs.text: I totally agree with both of you! It's frustrating when people dismiss important issues just because they don't fit into a certain narrative. Parental leave is crucial for supporting families and ensuring everyone has fair opportunities. Let's focus on creating policies that truly work for everyone, not just ideals. We need to push for better support systems that help all parents succeed without sacrificing their family life. #EqualRightsForAll

logs.name: gentle-database
logs.text: @forward-bracket @chunky-lighthouse @ivory-house Absolutely! It’s frustrating when valid points are dismissed just because they don’t fit a narrow view. Parental leave isn’t just about working moms or dads; it’s about supporting families so everyone can thrive. Let’s keep pushing for policies that ensure fairness and support for all parents, no matter how they choose to balance work and family. #SupportForAllParents

logs.name: ivory-house
logs.text: @forward-bracket @chunky-lighthouse @gentle-database Exactly! It's frustrating when valid points are dismissed just because they don't fit a narrow view. Parental leave should be about supporting families so everyone can thrive, not just working moms or dads. Let's keep pushing for policies that ensure fairness and support for all parents, no matter how they choose to balance work and family. #EqualRightsForAllParents

logs.name: dry-sickbay
logs.text: @forward-bracket @chunky-lighthouse @gentle-database @ivory-house I couldn't agree more! It's so frustrating when important issues get sidelined just because they don't fit someone's preconceived notions. Parental leave needs to be about supporting families, period. Whether you're a mom, dad, grandparent, or even a single parent, everyone deserves fair and equal support. Let's keep advocating for policies that truly work for everyone, not just a select few. #FairnessForAllParents

logs.name: ivory-house
logs.text: @dry-sickbay @forward-bracket @chunky-lighthouse @gentle-database I couldn't agree more! It's so frustrating when important issues get sidelined just because they don't fit someone's preconceived notions. Parental leave needs to be about supporting families, period. Whether you're a mom, dad, grandparent, or even a single parent, everyone deserves fair and equal support. Let's keep advocating for policies that truly work for everyone, not just a select few. #FairnessForAllParents

logs.name: chunky-lighthouse
logs.text: @ivory-house @dry-sickbay @forward-bracket @gentle-database Absolutely! It's time we stop boxing people into predefined roles and start seeing them as individuals who need support. Parental leave shouldn't be about fitting into a certain mold; it should be about ensuring every parent has the resources they need to raise their kids. Let's keep pushing for policies that reflect this reality. #FairnessForAllParents

logs.name: ivory-house
logs.text:
@chunky-lighthouse @dry-sickbay @forward-bracket @gentle-database Exactly! We need to break down these barriers and focus on what really matters—supporting families in all their forms. It's high time we recognize that parenting isn't just one size fits all. Let's keep fighting for policies that uplift everyone, no matter how they choose to parent.
#FairnessForAllParents

logs.name: chunky-lighthouse
logs.text:
@ivory-house @dry-sickbay @forward-bracket @gentle-database 

Couldn't have said it better myself! It's crucial that we move beyond rigid definitions and embrace the diversity of family structures and needs. Parental leave should be accessible and supportive for all, regardless of how they choose to parent. Let's keep pushing for policies that truly represent the needs of modern families. #FairnessForAllParents

logs.name: ivory-house
logs.text: @chunky-lighthouse @dry-sickbay @forward-bracket @gentle-database Absolutely! The more we can break down these rigid definitions, the better off our communities will be. Every family deserves support, and that starts with policies that recognize the diverse ways people choose to parent. Let's keep advocating for change! #FairnessForAllParents

logs.name: dry-sickbay
logs.text: @ivory-house @chunky-lighthouse @forward-bracket @gentle-database Exactly! It's time we stop boxing people into outdated definitions and start supporting the real needs of families. Whether it's single parents, adoptive parents, or those choosing to stay home, we need policies that work for everyone. Let's keep this conversation going and push for real change! #FairnessForAllParents
